# Welcome

> Welcome to the AutoPilot documentation! This is your go-to resource for all things related to AutoPilot. Please note that our documentation is currently a work in progress.

- [Welcome](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/index.md): Welcome
- [Patches](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/index.md): Patches are categorized as Critical, Recommended, or Optional, indicating their severity and the urgency of application. This classification is reflected in the last character of the patch's name.

## Guides

- [Create A Custom Boot Hook](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/guides/custom-boot-hooks/index.md): If you ever need to run custom tasks during the boot process of your instances, AutoPilot provides a flexible system for adding custom boot hook scripts. These scripts allow you to execute specific commands or tasks at boot time, ensuring that your environment is configured exactly as needed.
- [Deploy Shopware With GitHub Actions](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/guides/deploy-shopware-with-github-actions/index.md): This guide takes you from having no deployment pipeline to a fully automated GitOps workflow for your Shopware store on JetRails AutoPilot. Using Deployer
- [Onboard Magento With Deployer](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/guides/onboard-magento-with-deployer/index.md): This guide will walk you through onboarding your existing Magento Store onto the AutoPilot platform using Deployer. We will initially deploy a sample codebase onto a new deployment.
- [Self-Hosted GitHub Action Runners](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/guides/self-hosted-github-action-runners/index.md): This guide walks you through setting up self-hosted GitHub Action runners using Actions Runner Controller (ARC) on a K3s server deployed through JetRails AutoPilot.
- [Serve Web Files on Root Filesystem](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/guides/serve-web-files-on-root-filesystem/index.md): Web files are accessible across your cluster deployment using NFS. While this provides a convenient way to share files, it can also introduce challenges, particularly around old code bases that still use modules that follow the PSR-0 standard.

## Patches

- [P001C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p001c.md): Fixes data persistence issue with OpenSearch and Redis.
- [P002R](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p002r.md): PHP performance improvements.
- [P003O](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p003o.md): Removed SSH keys count limit.
- [P004C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p004c.md): Daily Backups For RDS With Standard Retention
- [P005O](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p005o.md): Null-Bytes Within Rotated Log Files.
- [P006C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p006c.md): Atomic Writes When Scaling Web Tier
- [P007C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p007c.md): Issue Using Non-Default Varnish Version
- [P008C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p008c.md): Stop Tracking RDS Engine Version
- [P009C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p009c.md): Varnish Session Hijack Fix
- [P010O](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p010o.md): Install Composer 2.9.0
- [P011C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p011c.md): Default HTTPS Listener Certificate
- [P012C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p012c.md): PolyShell (CVE-2025-54236)
- [P013C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p013c.md): Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431)
- [P014C](https://docs.autopilot.jetrails.com/patches/p014c.md): Dirty Frag (CVE-2026-43284)
